title: The Vestal Virgins
description: A Roman writer explains the sacred duties and privileges of the Vestal Virgins.

latin_text.text: Vestālēs virginis sacrum ignem in templō Vestae custodiēbant. hic ignis
numquam extinctus esse dēbēbat, quod Rōmānī crēdēbant salutem urbis ab
eō pendēre. virginēs sex in ordinem lēctae erant, quae trīgintā annōs
in templō manēbant officiaque sacra faciēbant.
multī honōrēs eīs datī sunt. in theātrō et in circō locīs honorātīs sedēbant.
sī Vestālis in viā occurrisset reō damnātō, ille statim līberābātur.
virgō autem quae ignem neglexisset aut castitātem violāvisset gravissimē
pūniēbātur. vīva in terrā sepulta morerētur.

vocabulary_notes.meaning: Vestal Virgins (priestesses of Vesta, goddess of the hearth)
the safety/welfare of the city (accusative of salūs)
depended on it (infinitive of pendeō, for indirect statement)
had been chosen into the order/college
would be buried alive and would die (imperfect subjunctive)

model_translation.text: The Vestal Virgins were guarding the sacred fire in the temple of Vesta. This fire was never supposed to be extinguished, because the Romans believed that the safety of the city depended on it. Six virgins had been chosen into the order, who remained in the temple for thirty years and performed sacred duties.

Many honours were given to them. In the theatre and in the circus they sat in places of honour. If a Vestal were to encounter a condemned criminal on the road, he was immediately freed. However, a virgin who had neglected the fire or had violated her chastity was punished very severely. She would die buried alive in the ground.

key_phrases: the Romans believed that the safety of the city depended on it
had been chosen into the order
remained in the temple for thirty years
Many honours were given to them
If a Vestal were to encounter a condemned criminal
had neglected the fire or had violated her chastity
She would die buried alive in the ground
